text: It's true name is Erasmus' Razzle Dazzle, by the way.
You cast the spell and anyone looking directly at its corruscating light is blinded for awhile and can't do anything.
Magical traps are a good way to keep thieves out of your chests and other places.
Put a fire trap, for example, on a chest.  When someone tries to open it, the thief will go up in smoke!
I only got singed the last time.  Nothing major.
A simple yes or no will suffice.
Come in and sit down.
Fenrus, our guest has arrived.
Since you are a practitioner of the magical arts, you might be interested in a little game I have.
I'll teach you the Dazzle spell if you win.  You do want to play, don't you?
Nice to see you back.  Shall we play a game of Mage's Maze?
Wonderful!  Then you can play a game of Mage's Maze with me.
Oh, well.  It is a good game.  Good practice, too.  Maybe we can play the next time you visit.
I believe I gave a learning scroll to the Hermit.  You should talk to him about it.
Too bad.  You really can't play the game without it.  You can buy a learning scroll at Zara's magic shop in town.
I so enjoy playing the Mage's Maze.
YUK-YUK-YUK-GUFFAW!
A-HYUK, A-HYUK, A-HYUK.  GAWRSH!
Tee-Hee-Hee!

HO-HO-HO-HEE-HEE-HEE
 HA-HA-HA!
The only thing I've seen the brigand warlock cast is sneezing powder.
It's important for a magic user to know as many spells as possible.  You never know what might come in handy.
They have a supposed warlock working with them.  From what I've seen, he's more nincompoop than necromancer.
I am a wizard of some reknown.
Please!  The least you could do is ask me about something more interesting.
I told you to be more interesting!  You may go now.

You seem to know all of my punchlines, so...
to SPELL with you!
Not in MY house you don't...
Well, if you really must go...
"Too much for you?  I guess you should work on your magic.  Maybe next time you'll be more of a challenge."
"Guess I still have a knack for the old Maze game."
"As I promised, I shall now teach you the spell known as `Erasmus' Razzle Dazzle'."
"Well!  You don't have to rub it in, you know!"
